Kirkland & Ellis LLP Defined Benefit Retirement Benefit (value not readily ascertainable)

I resigned from Kirkland & Ellis LLP in February 2018 after 25 years of service at the firm, which triggered an entitlement to benefits.  I began receiving monthly payments in September 2019.  The current monthly benefit, to which I am entitled for the rest of my life and my spouse's life (whichever comes later), is $31,528.42.  This amount is adjusted for inflation, and hence will presumably increase over time.

I resigned from Kirkland & Ellis LLP effective February 28, 2018, and became eligible to participate in a defined benefits plan (the Partners Unfunded Retirement Plan (PURP)) in August 2019. As of then, I began receiving fixed monthly retirement benefits (adjusted annually for cost of living), which now amount to $31,528.42/month. (2018-02-01)

I will retain an interest in one contingency case being litigation by Ellis George LLP.  If the client were to ultimately prevail om appeal, I am entitled to receive a portion of future recovery in the case based on a maximum set percentage set forth in an agreement with the firm. (2023-06-01)
I will continue to participate in the firm's 401(k) plan.  the plan sponsor will not make further contributions after my separation. (2021-11-01)
